requirement_title: Operational Authorisation Pack Completeness and Case Opening
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Workbench Artefact Detector shall, upon applicant submission of an Operational Authorisation Pack, execute automated validation against the mandatory-artefact list (ConOps, SORA, Safety Case, Training Plan, Personnel List, Environmental Assessment, Insurance, Ground Survey) within 10 minutes of upload. Output: Red/Amber/Green coverage matrix. Pack shall be marked GREEN when all 8 mandatory artefacts are present, validly formatted (PDF/XLSX/DOCX), and exceed minimum page thresholds: ConOps >= 5 pages, SORA >= 3 pages, Safety Case >= 10 pages. A complete pack shall cause the case to enter open-case state and advance to SORA Assessment. An incomplete pack shall cause return-for-clarification state with explicit specification of missing artefacts. Test shall be run on 50 representative regulatory submission samples (Scotland-pattern and CTRL-ALT-DEFEAT-pattern regression sets) from the DeFOSPAM scenarios; pass criterion: 100% of samples correctly identified and marked with paragraph-level pointers.
pass_threshold: 100% detection accuracy across 50 sample packs; all 8 mandatory artefacts correctly identified; case state transition executed correctly (open-case for complete, return-for-clarification for incomplete); processing time <= 10 minutes p95
sample_size_or_coverage: 50 representative packs (mix of complete/incomplete, classification levels OFFICIAL and OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE); regression suite includes Scotland-pattern (baseline VLOS) and CTRL-ALT-DEFEAT-pattern (complex swarm) submissions
test_owner: Workbench QA / CAA case officer (manual verification of complex cases)
anchor_meter: METER-001 (Artefact Detector Quick Gate), METER-002 (Paragraph-Level Coverage Audit)
anchor_sla: NFR-LAT-002 (Evidence upload throughput), NFR-LAT-003 (Coverage matrix generation within 10 min)
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM Dorothy-8 (vague 'complete' qualifier). Operationalized through: (1) explicit artefact enumeration (8 types), (2) format validation rules, (3) page-count thresholds, (4) timed output (10 min), (5) state transition verification. REQ001 and REQ012 both use 'complete' but in different senses: REQ001 = artefact presence, REQ012 = evidentiary continuity. Separated here for clarity.

requirement_title: Swarm-Aware SORA Evidence and SAIL/OSO Derivation
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The SORA Assessor shall, when evaluating a BVLOS swarm trial, review the SORA evidence and extract all risk items (hazards, failure modes, controls). For each risk item, assessor shall tag whether it explicitly addresses swarm-specific failure modes: (1) Coordinated-link-loss cascades, (2) Emergent misbehavior (deadlock, oscillation), (3) Unintended inter-vehicle interactions. Target: >= 3 distinct swarm-aware risk items per aircraft, distributed across the three categories. Each item must cite supporting evidence (test report, design review, simulation data). Evidence shall be defensible through reference to JARUS SORA v2.5 methodology and documented hazard analysis (FTA or STAMP). Upon evaluation, the assessor shall issue SAIL (Specific Assurance and Integrity Level) and OSO (Operational Safety Objectives) targets explicitly referencing swarm coordination constraints. Test shall be run on 20 representative BVLOS swarm SORA packages; pass criterion: 100% of SORA packages contain >= 3 swarm-aware risk items per aircraft with evidence citations, and derived SAIL/OSO explicitly reference swarm coordination (e.g., 'Separation maintained during coordinated evasion >= 50 m horizontal, 20 m vertical').
pass_threshold: 100% of SORA packages contain >= 3 swarm-aware risk items distributed across 3 categories; 100% include evidence citations (test reports, FTA, STAMP); 100% of derived SAIL/OSO explicitly reference swarm constraints with numerical bounds
sample_size_or_coverage: 20 representative BVLOS swarm SORA packages; mix of single-swarm (2-4 aircraft) and large-swarm (5+ aircraft) scenarios; mix of mission types (coordinated evasion, formation flight, search-and-rescue)
test_owner: CAA SORA Assessor (human domain expert)
anchor_meter: METER-003 (Swarm-Specific Risk Evidence Inventory Audit)
anchor_sla: None directly; but REQ002 acceptance gates entry to REQ007 (DAA/de-confliction evidence)
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM Dorothy-1, Dorothy-3. 'Swarm-aware' operationalized as 3-component evidence framework (coordinated-link-loss, emergent-misbehavior, inter-vehicle interactions). 'Defensibly' operationalized through JARUS v2.5 + documented hazard analysis. Vague qualifier removed; inspection process is now repeatable and scored binary (pass >= 3 items or fail).

requirement_title: Clear ConOps and ODD for Nominal and Worst-Credible Behaviour Assessment
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Safety Case Reviewer shall receive a Concept of Operations (ConOps) document specifying: (1) Swarm composition and roles, (2) Operational phases with crew decision points, (3) Failure modes and contingency responses, (4) Nominal behavior thresholds (e.g., normal flight plan deviation < 2 degrees, descent rate < 100 ft/min). The Reviewer shall also receive the Operational Design Domain (ODD) specifying operating conditions (wind speed <= 15 knots, temperature -5°C to +30°C, GPS accuracy better than 5 meters, daylight hours only). Worst-credible behavior is defined as the most severe single-failure scenario reasonably foreseeable (e.g., loss of GPS signal, C2 link loss, geofence breach) that system and crew must survive. Upon review, the Reviewer shall validate that all worst-credible behavior scenarios are addressed by documented crew procedures or containment systems. Reviewer shall issue accept, return-for-clarification, or refuse-with-reasons decision. Test shall be run on 15 representative ConOps and ODD documents; pass criterion: 100% of documents contain all 4 required ConOps sections with nominally-specified thresholds, all required ODD environmental bounds are numerically specified, and >= 90% of credible single-failure scenarios are addressed by documented procedures.
pass_threshold: 100% ConOps documents contain all 4 required sections; 100% ODD documents specify numeric environmental bounds for wind, temperature, GPS accuracy, lighting; >= 90% of credible single-failure scenarios addressed by documented procedures or containment systems
sample_size_or_coverage: 15 representative ConOps/ODD document pairs; mix of VLOS (baseline) and BVLOS (single and swarm) scenarios; mix of failure scenarios (link loss, GPS loss, geofence breach, crew incapacitation)
test_owner: CAA Safety Case Reviewer (human inspector)
anchor_meter: METER-002 (Paragraph-Level Coverage Audit for ConOps section completeness)
anchor_sla: None directly; but ConOps clarity gates entry to REQ005 (automatic containment design review)
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM Dorothy-5. 'Clear' operationalized through enumeration of 4 required ConOps sections. 'Worst-credible behaviour' now scoped to single-failure assumption (not common-mode or software defects per Chris's definition). 'Judge' (subjective verb) replaced with 'validate against documented procedures' (testable). Acceptance criterion is now binary: >= 90% coverage of credible failures or fail.

requirement_title: Temporary Danger Area and Consultation Evidence Package
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall submit a Temporary Danger Area (TDA) request to the Airspace Regulator specifying: (1) 3D airspace volume (latitude, longitude, altitude bands, time window in ICAO format), (2) NOTAM text formatted per ICAO Annex 15 for publication to civilian traffic, (3) Coordination evidence showing documented consultation with ATSU (Air Traffic Service Unit), airspace users (e.g., commercial routes), and affected landowners. The Airspace Regulator shall review and verify: (A) TDA boundaries do not overlap with published airways, restricted airspace, or active training routes (checked against national airspace database), (B) ATSU has confirmed capacity to issue NOTAM and monitor boundary compliance, (C) Duration and re-entry procedures are documented. Upon approval, Regulator shall authorize TDA publication. Upon rejection, Regulator shall specify which elements (3D volume, NOTAM text, or coordination evidence) require revision. Test shall be run on 10 representative TDA submissions; pass criterion: 100% of requests contain properly-formatted 3D volume specifications, valid ICAO Annex 15 NOTAM text, and documented evidence of consultation with all 3 stakeholder groups (ATSU, airspace users, landowners).
pass_threshold: 100% of TDA requests contain: proper 3D airspace volume specification (latitude, longitude, altitude, time), valid ICAO Annex 15 NOTAM text, documented evidence of consultation with 3 stakeholder groups (ATSU, commercial routes, landowners); 100% verified against national airspace database (zero overlaps with published airways/restricted airspace)
sample_size_or_coverage: 10 representative TDA submissions; mix of geographic locations (rural Scotland, UK Midlands, coastal areas, airspace with commercial traffic); mix of duration/complexity (1-day trials, multi-week campaigns)
test_owner: CAA Airspace Regulator / ATSU liaison
anchor_meter: None directly; but TDA validation gates entry to REQ011 (ATSU coordination for independent termination)
anchor_sla: None directly
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM observation on passive voice ('segregate' with unclear mechanism). Odin rewrite specifies explicit actors (Applicant submits, Regulator reviews, ATSU enforces). 'Consultation package' operationalized as documented evidence of consultation with 3 stakeholder groups. Acceptance is binary: all 3 stakeholders documented or fail.

requirement_title: Automatic Containment Systems for Geofence Enforcement
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The UAS shall be equipped with automatic containment systems (geofence monitor + primary containment action) that prevent egress beyond the geofence (defined 3D boundary: latitude ±tolerance, longitude ±tolerance, altitude floor/ceiling) independent of C2 link state or onboard autonomy. Containment shall combine: (1) Geofence monitor running onboard, polling GNSS at 10 Hz minimum, (2) Primary containment action (priority: Return-to-Launch if GPS available; fallback: climb to safe altitude and hold). Upon geofence breach detection (aircraft position exceeds boundary by > 10 meters for > 2 seconds), system shall: (A) Trigger containment action within 500 milliseconds, (B) Log breach event (timestamp, coordinates, breach direction), (C) Notify crew via alert (audio + visual) within 1 second. Containment design shall be validated through: (1) Software testing (HITL simulation, boundary stress testing with worst-case GPS noise + wind), (2) Flight testing (minimum 10 flights with deliberate near-breach scenarios), (3) CAA autonomy reviewer approval before trial authorization. Test shall be run on 10 representative UAS models; pass criterion: 100% of breach scenarios trigger containment action within 500 ms, 100% of breaches are logged, 100% of crew alerts are delivered within 1 second; boundary margin testing shows robust operation even with +/- 15m GPS uncertainty.
pass_threshold: 100% of simulated geofence breach scenarios result in containment action within 500 ms; 100% of breaches logged with timestamp/coordinates; 100% of crew alerts delivered within 1 second; robust operation confirmed under +/- 15m GPS noise (worst-case uncertainty); HITL simulator passes and flight test passes (10 flights, zero uncontained breaches)
sample_size_or_coverage: 10 representative UAS models; HITL testing: 100 breach scenarios per model (varied GPS noise +/- 15m, wind 0-20 knots, boundary approach angles 0-360 degrees); Flight testing: minimum 10 flights per UAS model with deliberate near-breach and boundary-stress scenarios
test_owner: Applicant autonomy team (HITL + flight test), CAA autonomy reviewer (approval gate)
anchor_meter: None directly from Vibe; but operationalization is similar to METER-001 (automated detection with state-transition verification)
anchor_sla: None directly; but containment latency budget (500 ms) must be reserved in airframe control loop design (e.g., servo loop < 100 ms)
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM Dorothy-4, Observation-1 on critical safety gap. 'Containment' operationalized with 3 design components (monitor, action, alert), specific response times (500 ms action, 1 s alert), and margin testing (15m GPS noise). Critical safety requirement now has testable acceptance: deterministic response time, 100% action execution, robust margin. Failure of containment is grounds for mandatory trial suspension.

requirement_title: Spectrum-Authorized C2 and Inter-Vehicle Links with Link-Loss Survivability
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall obtain spectrum authorization from Spectrum Regulator for: (1) C2 (Command and Control) link frequency allocation and power limits, (2) Inter-vehicle link frequency allocation (for swarm aircraft-to-aircraft coordination). Authorization requires evidence that link design is: (A) Non-interfering: power spectral density meets ITU-R recommendations, validated through EMC testing (radiated emissions per EN 61000-6-4), (B) Monitored: real-time link quality telemetry (RSSI, BER) transmitted to ground crew and logged. Upon C2 link loss (signal quality drops below minimum threshold for > 1 second), aircraft shall execute failover: (1) If GPS available, maintain altitude and execute Return-to-Launch trajectory, (2) If GPS unavailable, climb to safe altitude (500 feet AGL minimum) and hold. Swarm coordination during C2 loss shall rely on inter-vehicle links and pre-programmed contingency responses. Survival time (duration crew can maintain safe state without ground C2) shall be demonstrated through simulation and flight test, with acceptance criterion: minimum 5 minutes for BVLOS swarm (allow crew time to re-establish C2 or execute independent termination). Test shall be run on 8 representative link configurations (mix of frequency bands, antenna types, swarm sizes); pass criterion: 100% of EMC tests pass ITU-R limits, 100% of link-loss scenarios achieve >= 5 minute survival time, telemetry RSSI/BER logs show continuous monitoring (zero > 2 second gaps).
pass_threshold: 100% EMC tests pass ITU-R power spectral density limits per EN 61000-6-4; 100% of C2 link-loss scenarios (loss for > 1 sec) trigger failover within 500 ms; 100% of failover maneuvers execute correctly (RTL if GPS available, climb-and-hold if not); survival time >= 5 minutes in >= 95% of trials; RSSI/BER telemetry continuous (zero > 2 second gaps)
sample_size_or_coverage: 8 representative link configurations; EMC testing: 20 radiated-emission test scenarios per configuration; Link-loss simulation: 50 loss-event scenarios per configuration (varied loss duration 1-30 sec, environmental noise, swarm size 2-10 aircraft); Flight testing: minimum 5 flights per configuration with induced C2 loss
test_owner: Spectrum Regulator (EMC authorization), Applicant link-design team (failover testing), CAA flight test observer
anchor_meter: None directly; but link-quality monitoring operationalization similar to METER-003 (evidence audit with explicit tagging)
anchor_sla: None directly; but failover latency (500 ms) should be validated against control loop timing (typically < 100 ms per REQ005)
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM Flo-1, Observation-2. 'Link loss survivable' operationalized through: (1) time threshold (loss > 1 sec), (2) failover actions (RTL or hold with specific altitude 500 ft AGL), (3) survival time acceptance (>= 5 min). 'Non-interfering' operationalized through ITU-R compliance + EMC testing. Acceptance is binary: >= 5 min survival in >= 95% of trials or fail. This closes critical gap on undefined 'survivable' outcome.

requirement_title: Detect-and-Avoid Evidence and Coordinated Swarm De-Confliction
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall provide DO-365 (Minimum Operational Performance Standards for Airborne Detect-and-Avoid Systems) and F3442-2 (Standard Practice for Unmanned Aircraft Systems Traffic Management at Low Altitudes) evidence demonstrating: (1) Each aircraft has onboard DAA sensor (radar or electro-optical) with minimum detection range 500 meters, update rate 1 Hz, (2) Swarm coordination uses a defined de-confliction algorithm (specify: centralized ground-based, distributed peer voting, or hierarchical lead-decides) with documented decision logic. For cooperative encounters (intruder transmits ADS-B or FLARM), algorithm shall compute relative bearing and range, issue recommended maneuvers (climb, descend, left turn, right turn) via inter-vehicle links, enforce agreed maneuver. For non-cooperative encounters (intruder does not transmit), algorithm shall issue crew-advisory maneuver and record decision rationale. Encounter resolution is predictable when: (A) Same encounter geometry always produces same response (deterministic, not probabilistic), (B) Response is deterministic (not probabilistic), (C) All swarm aircraft follow preferred maneuver within 2 seconds. Test shall be run on 100 representative encounter scenarios (20 cooperative, 20 non-cooperative, 60 multi-aircraft swarm encounters); pass criterion: 100% of encounter scenarios produce deterministic response, all swarm aircraft execute agreed maneuver within 2 seconds in >= 95% of trials, collision avoidance (separation >= 100 meters horizontally) maintained in 100% of trials.
pass_threshold: 100% DO-365 and F3442-2 requirements met per regulatory standard review; 100% of encounter scenarios produce deterministic response (same geometry = same maneuver); 100% of swarm aircraft execute agreed maneuver within 2 seconds in >= 95% of trials; collision avoidance (>= 100 m horizontal separation) maintained in 100% of trials; zero loss-of-separation incidents
sample_size_or_coverage: 100 representative encounter scenarios: 20 cooperative (ADS-B intruder), 20 non-cooperative (radar-only), 60 swarm multi-aircraft encounters; encounters in multiple geometries (head-on, crossing, overtake, coordinated evasion); swarm sizes 2-10 aircraft; simulation + flight test validation
test_owner: Applicant DAA system team (algorithm testing), CAA DAA assessor (compliance review)
anchor_meter: None directly; but de-confliction algorithm determinism verification similar to METER-003 (evidence audit with explicit pass criteria)
anchor_sla: None directly; but maneuver execution latency (2 sec) should be validated against swarm communication latency (typically < 500 ms per REQ006)
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM Paul-1, Observation-3. 'Resolved predictably' operationalized through 3-part criterion: (A) deterministic algorithm (not probabilistic), (B) same geometry = same response, (C) 2-second execution deadline. Algorithm type is specified via enumeration (3 options: centralized, distributed, hierarchical). Acceptance is binary: 100% deterministic response or fail. Failure (collision, loss of separation < 100 m) triggers trial suspension.

requirement_title: Swarm Decision-Making Architecture and Emergent-Behaviour Evidence
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall document the swarm decision-making architecture, specifying which of three design patterns applies: (1) Centralized (all decisions made by ground crew or ground-based autonomy agent), (2) Distributed (peer voting: each aircraft shares state, majority vote selects action), (3) Hierarchical (lead aircraft decides, followers execute). Documentation shall specify: (A) Decision authority and override rules, (B) Communication topology (mesh, star, line), (C) Failure modes (leader loss, communication loss, Byzantine aircraft). The Applicant shall provide emergent-behavior evidence through: (1) Formal verification (model checking or theorem proving) proving absence of undesired emergent behaviors (separation loss, deadlock, oscillation), (2) Monte Carlo simulation (minimum 1,000 trials per scenario) demonstrating behavior variance across environmental noise and sensor uncertainty, (3) HITL testing of swarm behaviors (formation flight, coordinated evasion, loss-of-leader recovery). For each scenario, applicant shall bound behavior variance (e.g., 'separation loss occurs in < 0.1% of trials'). Test shall be run on 10 representative swarm architectures; pass criterion: 100% of architectures have complete formal-verification proof or conservative model-checking bounds, all Monte Carlo simulations bound variance to <= 0.1% for safety-critical behaviors (separation loss, deadlock), >= 95% of HITL scenarios execute within predicted behavior envelope.
pass_threshold: 100% of architectures specify decision authority, communication topology, and failure modes; formal verification covers >= 95% of state space or model-checking establishes conservative bounds; Monte Carlo simulations (>= 1000 trials per scenario) bound variance to <= 0.1% for separation loss and deadlock; HITL testing shows >= 95% of scenarios within predicted behavior envelope
sample_size_or_coverage: 10 representative swarm architectures (mix of centralized, distributed, hierarchical); 50+ Monte Carlo scenarios per architecture; formal verification: >= 1000 model-checking states per architecture; HITL testing: >= 30 test scenarios per architecture (formation flight, evasion, leader loss, communication loss)
test_owner: Applicant autonomy team (formal verification, simulation, HITL), CAA autonomy reviewer (completeness assessment)
anchor_meter: None directly; but emergent-behavior bounding similar to METER-003 (evidence audit with explicit criteria)
anchor_sla: None directly
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM observation on undefined 'decision-making architecture' and 'emergent-behaviour.' Architecture is now scoped to 3 design patterns; failure modes explicitly required. Emergent behavior is operationalized through 3-tier evidence (formal + simulation + HITL) with variance bounds (0.1% for safety-critical behaviors). Acceptance is binary: formal + simulation + HITL all pass bounds or fail. This removes 'assure' (subjective) and replaces with 'bound and demonstrate' (verifiable).

requirement_title: ML/AI Assurance Case, Frozen-Model Manifest, and Explainability Evidence
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall provide three artifacts for ML Assurance: (1) AMLAS Case (Assurance Case for Machine Learning in Autonomous Systems per Assure ML CTC) documenting: safety requirements, training data governance, model architecture, robustness testing, failure modes; (2) Frozen-Model Manifest specifying for each ML model deployed: version ID, training dataset hash (SHA256), hyperparameters (learning rate, regularization), performance metrics (validation accuracy, F1 score for safety-critical classes), operational constraints (input domain, confidence thresholds), explicit confirmation that model weights and architecture remain immutable throughout trial; (3) Explainability Evidence demonstrating post-hoc explainability through: feature importance analysis (SHAP, LIME), decision-tree approximations, or mechanistic interpretability. Forensic explainability is not perfect reconstruction but rather: sufficient to identify input features contributing to decision, sufficient to identify if model operated within training distribution, sufficient to trace decision to model version per Manifest. Test shall be run on 8 representative ML models (mix of swarm de-confliction, emergency-decision ML); pass criterion: 100% of AMLAS cases contain all required elements per Assure ML CTC, 100% of Frozen-Model Manifests are immutable per code review (confirmed via cryptographic hash), 100% of models have >= 2 explainability methods deployed (e.g., SHAP + LIME), forensic explainability sufficient to identify feature contributions and training distribution for 100% of test decision instances.
pass_threshold: 100% AMLAS cases complete per Assure ML CTC (safety requirements, training data governance, model architecture, robustness testing, failure modes all documented); 100% Frozen-Model Manifests immutable throughout trial (confirmed via SHA256 verification and code review); 100% of models have >= 2 deployed explainability methods; forensic explainability demonstrated: feature attribution documented, training distribution verification possible, model version tracing enabled for 100% of decision instances
sample_size_or_coverage: 8 representative ML models; AMLAS completeness audit: 100% coverage of required case elements; Frozen-Model Manifest verification: cryptographic hash validation on all models; Explainability testing: 100 decision instances per model traced to features and training distribution
test_owner: CAA ML/AI Assurance Specialist (AMLAS review, Manifest verification), independent auditor (explainability testing)
anchor_meter: None directly; but AMLAS case review similar to METER-002 (section-level completeness audit)
anchor_sla: None directly
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM Dorothy-6, Dorothy-7. 'Forensically explainable' reframed as 'sufficient for investigation, not perfect reconstruction.' Operationalized through 3 techniques (SHAP, LIME, mechanistic), tied to Frozen-Model Manifest. 'Frozen-model manifest' now specifies 7 required fields (version, dataset hash, hyperparameters, metrics, constraints, immutability confirmation, hash verification). Acceptance is binary: AMLAS complete, Manifest immutable, >= 2 explainability methods or fail. Removes unrealistic 'perfect forensic reconstruction' standard.

requirement_title: Meaningful Human Control Demonstration Through Workload and Takeover Evidence
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall demonstrate meaningful human control through: (1) Continuous crew situational awareness (crew maintains awareness of swarm state: position, airspeed, altitude, attitude, link status, autonomous decisions) via frequent updates (minimum 1 Hz for each aircraft), (2) Retained authority (crew retains ability to override autonomous decisions and issue manual commands at all times, with override latency < 2 seconds), (3) Intervention capability (crew can perceive off-nominal events and intervene before safety-critical outcomes with time window >= 5 seconds for evasion maneuvers, >= 10 seconds for return-to-launch). Workload evidence shall quantify crew demands (number of concurrent decisions, decision frequency, time budget available) per flight phase. Crew workload shall not exceed 75% of maximum cognitive capacity during nominal phases, 85% during contingency. Takeover evidence shall demonstrate: (A) Time-to-takeover (crew can resume full manual control within X seconds of decision, where X is phase-dependent: VLOS <= 5 sec, BVLOS single <= 10 sec, BVLOS swarm <= 15 sec), (B) Takeover training (crew completed minimum 10 practice takeovers in HITL simulator, each logged with scenario and instructor evaluation), (C) Takeover authority (crew has full 6-DOF control of swarm upon takeover). Test shall be run on 6 representative crew profiles (pilots with 500-5000 hours experience); pass criterion: 100% of nominal flights show crew workload <= 75%, 100% of contingency scenarios show crew workload <= 85%, 100% of takeover attempts achieve control within phase-dependent latency windows (VLOS <= 5 sec, BVLOS single <= 10 sec, BVLOS swarm <= 15 sec), 100% of crews complete >= 10 simulator takeover sessions with documented instructor sign-off.
pass_threshold: Crew workload (NASA-TLX or SWAT assessment) <= 75% nominal phases, <= 85% contingency phases, for 100% of test flights; situational awareness update rate >= 1 Hz maintained for 100% of flight duration; override latency < 2 seconds for 100% of override commands; time-to-takeover within phase-dependent limits (VLOS <= 5 sec, BVLOS single <= 10 sec, BVLOS swarm <= 15 sec) for 100% of takeover attempts; >= 10 documented simulator sessions per crew with instructor sign-off
sample_size_or_coverage: 6 representative crew profiles (pilots with 500-1000, 1000-2000, 2000-5000 hours); 30+ nominal flight tests (10 per crew profile level); 30+ contingency scenario tests (takeover, link-loss, off-nominal); 60+ simulator takeover sessions (10 per crew member minimum)
test_owner: Applicant human-factors team (workload measurement), CAA flight test observer (takeover assessment), independent HITL simulator operator
anchor_meter: None directly; but workload assessment similar to METER-002 (subjective + objective criteria combined)
anchor_sla: None directly; but intervention time window (5-10 sec) must be validated against autonomy decision latency (typically < 1 sec per REQ008)
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM Dorothy-2. 'Meaningful human control' operationalized through 3 components: (1) awareness (1 Hz updates), (2) authority (< 2 sec override latency), (3) intervention capability (5-10 sec time window). 'Throughout' (temporal vagueness) resolved through per-phase acceptance criteria (75% nominal, 85% contingency). Workload thresholds are verifiable via NASA-TLX (objective instrument). Takeover latency is phase-dependent and measurable. Acceptance is binary: all three components met or fail.

requirement_title: Independent Flight Termination and Contingency Matrix with ATSU Coordination
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall implement Independent Flight Termination (IFT) system capable of forcing immediate cessation of flight independent of C2 link, inter-vehicle links, or onboard autonomy. IFT shall: (1) Trigger via dedicated ground-based RF kill-switch (crew-operated) or onboard geofence breach (automatic), (2) Effect termination within 500 milliseconds via engine cutoff or parachute deployment, (3) Operate when all other links are lost (fail-safe design verified through architecture review and ground testing). Upon IFT activation, crew shall: (A) Immediately alert ATSU and other airspace users via radio, (B) Preserve all telemetry and decision logs (5 seconds pre-termination, during, and 30 seconds post), (C) Recover or locate all aircraft and evidence. The Applicant shall provide a Contingency Matrix (table format) mapping off-nominal states to response procedures. Off-nominal states include: GPS loss, C2 link loss, geofence breach, airspace intrusion, crew incapacitation, loss of separation, low battery. For each state, matrix shall specify: autonomous action (Return-to-Launch, climb and hold, autonomous evasion), crew procedure (override authority, timing, fallback), and IFT trigger criteria. ATSU coordination evidence shall document: (1) Pre-trial briefing (ATSU understands trial operations and contingency procedures, signed confirmation), (2) Real-time monitoring (ATSU monitors TDA and notifies crew of airspace conflicts), (3) Emergency procedure (ATSU can declare TDA closed and issue immediate landing directive). Test shall be run on 10 representative IFT configurations; pass criterion: 100% of IFT activations achieve termination within 500 ms, 100% of activations are logged and recoverable, 100% of Contingency Matrices contain all 7 off-nominal states with specified autonomous/crew/IFT actions, ATSU pre-trial briefing and real-time monitoring confirmed via signed documentation.
pass_threshold: 100% IFT activations (RF kill-switch and automatic geofence-breach triggers) achieve termination within 500 ms; 100% of activations logged with pre/during/post telemetry (5 sec before, 30 sec after); 100% Contingency Matrices complete (all 7 off-nominal states with specified actions); 100% ATSU pre-trial briefings conducted with signed confirmation; real-time ATSU monitoring established (confirmed via TDA activation procedure documentation); ATSU emergency landing directive authority confirmed
sample_size_or_coverage: 10 representative IFT configurations (mix of RF kill-switch, parachute, engine-cutoff mechanisms); 50+ IFT activation tests (ground tests, HITL, flight tests with parachute recovery); Contingency Matrix validation: 10 representative matrices covering 7 off-nominal states; ATSU coordination: documented pre-trial briefings with 5+ representative ATSU facilities, real-time monitoring logs from 5+ flights
test_owner: Applicant IFT design team (mechanism testing), CAA flight test observer (flight validation), ATSU liaison officer (coordination verification)
anchor_meter: None directly; but IFT response-time verification similar to REQ005 (deterministic latency testing)
anchor_sla: IFT termination latency (500 ms) must be validated against geofence monitor (10 Hz polling) and servo loop (< 100 ms per REQ005)
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM observations on undefined 'independent termination' mechanism and 'contingency matrix' format. IFT now operationalized with 3 trigger modes (RF kill-switch, automatic geofence breach, crew procedure) and 500 ms response-time requirement. Contingency Matrix format specified as table with 3 columns (autonomous action, crew procedure, IFT trigger). Off-nominal states enumerated (7 types). ATSU coordination operationalized through 3-step evidence (pre-briefing, real-time monitoring, emergency landing authority). Acceptance is binary: IFT within 500 ms or fail.

requirement_title: Comprehensive Recorded Evidence, MOR Obligations, and Data Protection
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall implement telemetry and logging systems to record comprehensive evidence throughout trial. Evidence shall include: (1) Aircraft state (position, velocity, altitude, attitude) at 10 Hz, (2) Autonomy decisions (command issued, decision rationale) at 1 Hz, (3) C2 and inter-vehicle link quality (RSSI, BER) at 1 Hz, (4) Crew actions (control inputs, alerts acknowledged, overrides) logged with timestamp, (5) Video (forward-looking camera, downlink video) at minimum 30 fps, (6) Audio (crew intercom, radio transmissions) sampled at 16 kHz. All evidence shall be time-synchronized (GPS time reference), stored in tamper-evident containers (cryptographic hash SHA256 stored separately), retained for minimum 2 years. Recorded evidence is complete when all 6 categories present and continuous throughout flight (< 100 ms gaps acceptable). The Applicant shall fulfill Mandatory Occurrence Report (MOR) obligations by: (1) Identifying MOR-reportable events (accidents, serious incidents, injuries, structural damage, loss of separation < 100 m, unintended geofence breach, autonomous decision override), (2) Compiling incident narrative and recorded evidence within 72 hours of event, (3) Submitting to CAA Safety Investigation department. The Applicant shall conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) per GDPR Article 35 identifying: (A) Personal data collected (crew identities, observer photographs, tracked ground personnel), (B) Processing purposes (safety investigation, regulatory oversight), (C) Retention periods, (D) Data subject rights (access, deletion). DPIA shall document privacy controls (footage anonymization, access restrictions, deletion procedures) and obtain CAA Data Protection Officer review. Test shall be run on 5 representative trials; pass criterion: 100% of flights have all 6 evidence categories recorded continuously (< 100 ms gaps), 100% evidence integrity verified via SHA256 hashing, 100% MOR-reportable events correctly identified and submitted within 72 hours, 100% DPIA documented and approved by CAA Data Protection Officer.
pass_threshold: 100% of trial flights contain all 6 evidence categories (aircraft state, autonomy decisions, link quality, crew actions, video, audio) recorded continuously with < 100 ms gaps; 100% evidence time-synchronized via GPS reference; 100% evidence integrity verified via SHA256 hash stored separately; 100% MOR-reportable events correctly identified per regulatory definition; 100% of MOR submissions completed within 72 hours of event; 100% DPIA documented with privacy controls (anonymization, access restrictions, deletion procedures) and CAA DPO approval
sample_size_or_coverage: 5 representative trials; mix of VLOS, BVLOS single, BVLOS swarm; mix of mission types (normal, contingency, incident scenarios); telemetry completeness audit: 100% coverage of all 6 categories across 5 trials; MOR scenario testing: 10+ simulated MOR-reportable events per trial (72-hour submission deadline tracked); DPIA completeness: one DPIA per trial, verified against GDPR Article 35 checklist
test_owner: Applicant data-logging team (telemetry validation), CAA Flight Safety Investigator (MOR review), CAA Data Protection Officer (DPIA approval)
anchor_meter: METER-002 (Paragraph-Level Coverage Audit) adapted for telemetry completeness; similar section-by-section verification method
anchor_sla: NFR-LAT-003 (Coverage matrix generation within 10 min) depends on evidence upload; evidence recording systems must not impede trial progress
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM observation on ambiguous 'complete' (different from REQ001). REQ012 'complete' = evidentiary completeness (all data types present), operationalized through 6-category enumeration with sampling rates (10 Hz aircraft, 1 Hz decisions, 30 fps video, 16 kHz audio). 'Complete recorded evidence' now scoped as 6 types with continuous recording (< 100 ms gaps). MOR trigger operationalized through enumeration of 7 reportable event types per UK regulations. DPIA requirement operationalized through GDPR Article 35 checklist. Acceptance is binary: all 6 categories recorded and continuous or fail.

requirement_title: Third-Party Liability Insurance and Ground Environment Survey
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall obtain third-party liability insurance covering bodily injury, property damage, and environmental harm caused by UAS operations, with minimum coverage limits: 1 million GBP per occurrence, 5 million GBP annual aggregate. Insurance policy shall be underwritten by CAA-approved insurer and shall not exclude BVLOS or swarm operations. Certificate of insurance shall be provided prior to trial start. The Applicant shall commission an independent Ground Environment Survey assessing the trial area within 10 km radius of planned operations. Survey shall identify and document: (1) Ground infrastructure (power lines, communication towers, railways, roads) with GPS coordinates and criticality classification, (2) Population density (buildings, schools, hospitals, campgrounds) by district, estimated occupancy, (3) Environmental sensitivity (water sources, protected habitats, cultural heritage) per regulatory register, (4) Noise-sensitive receptors (residences, hospitals, schools) identified by occupancy, (5) Wind patterns and terrain features affecting aircraft behavior. Survey shall be conducted by licensed surveyor or environmental consultant and signed with certification that findings are factually grounded (based on observed site conditions, not theoretical models). For each identified ground hazard, applicant shall specify mitigation (reduced altitude, noise baffles, flight path adjustment, observer deployment) or risk acceptance rationale. Test shall be run on 5 representative trial areas; pass criterion: 100% of applications contain valid Certificate of Insurance from CAA-approved insurer with minimum coverage (1M per occurrence, 5M aggregate), 100% of applications contain signed Ground Survey (licensed surveyor signature), 100% of identified hazards have specified mitigation or documented risk acceptance, survey completeness >= 90% (all 5 categories present and substantive).
pass_threshold: 100% of trial applications contain valid Insurance Certificate from CAA-approved insurer with >= 1M GBP per occurrence and >= 5M GBP annual aggregate; 100% exclude BVLOS/swarm from exclusions; 100% of ground surveys signed by licensed surveyor or environmental consultant; 100% of surveys contain all 5 required categories (infrastructure, population density, environmental sensitivity, noise receptors, wind/terrain); >= 90% of identified hazards have specified mitigation or documented risk acceptance rationale
sample_size_or_coverage: 5 representative trial areas (mix of rural/suburban/urban locations, mix of hazard density - sparse to complex); insurance verification: policy document review for all 5 trial applications; ground survey verification: licensed surveyor credentials checked, all 5 categories audited for completeness and substantive content (>= 2 pages per category minimum)
test_owner: CAA Authorisation Officer (insurance verification), independent licensed surveyor (survey conduct and certification), CAA Authorisation Officer (hazard mitigation review)
anchor_meter: METER-002 (Paragraph-Level Coverage Audit) adapted for survey section completeness
anchor_sla: None directly; but ground survey must be completed >= 30 days before trial authorization to allow time for mitigation planning
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM observation on undefined 'factually grounded' and 'third-party risk' calculation. 'Factually grounded' operationalized through licensed surveyor certification (based on observed conditions, not theory). 'Third-party risk transferable' operationalized through specific insurance minimums (1M/5M GBP). Ground survey now specifies 5 required categories (infrastructure, population, environmental, noise receptors, wind/terrain). Acceptance is binary: valid Insurance Certificate + complete Ground Survey with licensed certification or fail.

requirement_title: Evidenced Crew Competency for BVLOS Swarm Operations
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall provide documented crew qualifications demonstrating competency across three domains: (1) Airmanship: minimum private pilot license or equivalent, current medical certificate, minimum 500 flight hours (VLOS or BVLOS), (2) BVLOS Swarm Operations: completion of approved training program (minimum 20 hours instruction) covering swarm behavior, coordination failures, contingency procedures, manual takeover; (3) System-Specific Knowledge: completion of type-specific training for designated UAS model covering launch/recovery, autonomy modes, C2 link management, emergency procedures. Evidence of competency shall be documented through: (A) Training Records: signed certificates from approved training organization, dated within 2 years, (B) Examination: scored assessment (minimum 80% pass) covering swarm operations, contingency procedures, system knowledge, (C) Simulator Practice: minimum 10 practice sessions in HITL simulator, each logged with date, scenario, instructor evaluation, (D) Flight Experience: logbook entries documenting BVLOS or swarm flights with designated supervising pilot if required. Licensing Inspector shall review evidence and issue: (1) Permit (all requirements met, crew approved for trial), (2) Conditional Permit (requirements substantially met, remedial training required), (3) Deny (material gaps). Test shall be run on 8 representative crew profiles; pass criterion: 100% of crew members demonstrate all three competency domains through submitted evidence, 100% meet specific credential requirements (500 hrs airmanship, 20 hrs BVLOS/swarm, system-specific training), 100% pass examination (>= 80%), 100% complete >= 10 simulator sessions with documented instructor sign-off, licenses and certificates valid (within 2-year renewal period).
pass_threshold: 100% of crew members possess: valid private pilot license or equivalent + current medical certificate + >= 500 flight hours (VLOS/BVLOS); 100% completed >= 20-hour BVLOS/swarm training program from approved organization; 100% completed system-specific training for designated UAS type; 100% passed written examination (>= 80%); 100% completed >= 10 documented HITL simulator sessions with instructor sign-off; all training records and certificates dated within 2 years; licenses/certificates valid for trial duration
sample_size_or_coverage: 8 representative crew profiles (mix of experience levels: 500-1000, 1000-2500, 2500+ flight hours; mix of background: rotorcraft, fixed-wing, helicopters); competency audit: credentials review for all 8 profiles; simulator session verification: 10+ sessions per crew member with instructor feedback documented; examination validation: >= 80% pass documented
test_owner: CAA Licensing Inspector (competency review and approval), approved training organization (training delivery and sign-off), HITL simulator operator (session logging and instructor evaluation)
anchor_meter: METER-002 (Paragraph-Level Coverage Audit) adapted for training-record completeness
anchor_sla: None directly
notes: Closes DeFOSPAM observation on undefined 'evidenced competency.' 'Qualified' operationalized through 3-domain framework (Airmanship 500 hrs, BVLOS/swarm 20 hrs, System-Specific training). Evidence is operationalized through 4 types (Training Records, Exam 80%, Simulator 10 sessions, Flight logbook). All credentials must be within 2-year renewal window. Acceptance is binary: all three domains met + all 4 evidence types submitted + exam passed (80%+) or fail. Conditional Permit allows remedial training pathway.

requirement_title: CAA/MAA Regulatory Apportionment for Classified MOD Trials
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: When a trial involves classified MOD (Ministry of Defence) evidence or military applications, both CAA (Civil Aviation Authority) and MAA (Military Aviation Authority) shall co-authorize the trial. The Applicant shall document a Regulatory Apportionment Matrix specifying: (1) Which regulatory requirements (REQ001-REQ020) each authority owns, (2) Decision dependencies (which authority approves first), (3) Information barriers (which evidence classified vs. shareable), (4) Escalation procedures (if CAA and MAA requirements conflict). Apportionment Matrix shall be reviewed and signed by designated representatives of both CAA and MAA, confirming: (A) All regulatory requirements (REQ001-REQ020) assigned to at least one authority (gap-free coverage), (B) No requirement left unassigned, (C) Both authorities acknowledge their approval responsibilities and authority to enforce conditions. Regulatory ownership is gap-free when every requirement REQ001-REQ020 is explicitly owned by either CAA or MAA (or jointly), and both authorities have signed. Upon execution, CAA/MAA liaison shall document approval and establish joint oversight mechanisms (e.g., shared evidence portal, joint review gates). Test shall be run on 5 representative classified MOD trials; pass criterion: 100% of trials have complete Apportionment Matrix covering all REQ001-REQ020, 100% of requirements assigned without gaps, 100% signed by designated CAA and MAA representatives, joint oversight mechanisms established and documented prior to trial authorization.
pass_threshold: 100% Regulatory Apportionment Matrices complete (all REQ001-REQ020 assigned); 100% gap-free coverage (every requirement assigned to CAA, MAA, or jointly); 100% signed by designated CAA and MAA representatives; 100% decision dependencies and information barriers specified; 100% escalation procedures documented for conflicting requirements; joint oversight mechanisms (shared evidence portal, joint review gates) established and operational
sample_size_or_coverage: 5 representative classified MOD trials (mix of military/civilian applications, mix of classification levels UK OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE to UK SECRET, mix of technologies - air/ground defense, autonomous swarms, sensor fusion)
test_owner: CAA/MAA liaison officer (Apportionment Matrix review and sign-off), CAA Director + MAA Director (executive approval)
anchor_meter: None directly; administrative compliance task rather than technical verification
anchor_sla: Apportionment Matrix must be finalized >= 30 days before trial authorization to establish joint oversight mechanisms
notes: REQ015 missing from original user stories. Operationalized through Regulatory Apportionment Matrix with 4 components (authority ownership, decision dependencies, information barriers, escalation). Gap-free coverage verified through checklist: all REQ001-REQ020 assigned (20-point audit). Acceptance is binary: all 20 requirements assigned + CAA/MAA both signed or fail. This closes the governance gap for classified MOD trials and ensures clear decision authority.

requirement_title: Reversible Phased Trial Gates with Entry/Exit Criteria and Progression Controls
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The trial shall be structured in three reversible phases, each with entry and exit gates controlled by CAA Programme Manager: Phase 1 (VLOS Baseline): single aircraft, visual control, crew within 500 m. Entry gate: approval of ConOps, SORA assessment, insurance, crew credentials. Phase 2 (BVLOS Single): transition to BVLOS, 2+ aircraft, autonomous waypoint navigation, C2 link reliance. Entry gate: Phase 1 successful completion (>= 10 flights, zero incidents), BVLOS-specific equipment validation, C2 link reliability (99.5% uptime in Phase 1). Phase 3 (BVLOS Swarm): full multi-aircraft coordinated operations, inter-vehicle link coordination, distributed decision-making. Entry gate: Phase 2 successful completion (>= 20 flights, zero off-nominal unrecovered events), swarm decision-making validation, emergent-behavior bounded (< 0.1% separation-loss). Phase 1 Exit criteria: 2+ incidents in 10 flights -> revert to requirements refinement; Phase 2 Exit criteria: C2 link unreliability observed (uptime < 99.5%) -> redesign C2 system; Phase 3 Exit criteria: undesired emergent behavior detected -> halt and investigate. Reversal is reversible (progressing backward) when exit criterion triggered: evidence re-evaluated, contributing factors remediated, re-entry gate re-attempted (not automatic re-progression). Programme Manager documents reversal rationale and remediation plan in trial file. Test shall be run on 3 representative trial campaigns; pass criterion: 100% of phase transitions follow entry/exit gate criteria, all reversals properly documented with remediation plans, zero phase progressions without meeting entry gates, Programme Manager approval recorded for all gate decisions.
pass_threshold: 100% of phase entries meet documented gate criteria (Phase 1: ConOps/SORA/insurance/crew approved; Phase 2: >= 10 Phase 1 flights zero incidents + C2 validated; Phase 3: >= 20 Phase 2 flights zero unrecovered + swarm behavior bounded); 100% of exits trigger documented remediation (incidents -> requirements refinement, link failure -> C2 redesign, emergent behavior -> investigation); 100% of reversals documented with remediation plan + re-entry gate; Programme Manager approval recorded for all transitions; zero unauthorized phase progressions
sample_size_or_coverage: 3 representative trial campaigns (mix of outcomes: campaign 1 progresses all phases, campaign 2 reverts from Phase 2, campaign 3 encounters Phase 3 emergent behavior); gate audit: 9 total gate transitions across 3 campaigns (3 entry gates, 3 continuation gates, 3 potential exit scenarios); documentation review: all gate decisions + reversals documented in trial file
test_owner: CAA Programme Manager (gate decision authority), trial applicant team (phase execution), independent observer (gate criterion verification)
anchor_meter: None directly; administrative trial-structure task
anchor_sla: None directly; but gate cycle time (Phase 1 exit/re-entry) should not exceed 14 days to minimize trial disruption
notes: REQ016 operationalized through 3-phase trial structure (VLOS, BVLOS Single, BVLOS Swarm) with explicit entry/exit gates. Reversibility is now testable: triggering exit criteria documented, remediation completed, re-entry gate re-attempted. Acceptance is binary: all phase transitions meet gates + all reversals documented + Programme Manager approval recorded or fail. This removes vague 'reversible' and replaces with specific gate criteria and remediation process.

requirement_title: Public and Stakeholder Engagement per CAP 1616 and GDPR
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall conduct Public and Stakeholder Engagement per CAP 1616 and GDPR requirements prior to trial start. Engagement shall include: (1) Landowner Notification: identify landowners within 2 km of trial area (via land registry), deliver written notice (minimum 30 days prior to trial) explaining trial operations, noise levels, duration, emergency contact, complaint procedure, signed with proof of delivery; (2) Community Consultation: host public information session (or provide online portal if remote) describing trial objectives, aircraft specifications, noise estimates, flight schedule, safety measures. Community members invited to submit concerns (deadline 14 days after notice); (3) Media Relations: issue press release to local media (5+ days before trial start) describing trial, emphasizing safety measures and regulatory oversight. Designate single media contact and prepare response to potential concerns; (4) GDPR Compliance: publish Privacy Notice describing personal data collected (crew and observer identities, photographic evidence, potentially incidental recording of ground personnel), retention duration (2 years), data subject rights (access, deletion), CAA Data Protection Officer contact. Test shall be run on 5 representative trial areas; pass criterion: 100% of applications document landowner identification and delivery of notices >= 30 days prior, 100% conduct community consultation (event or online portal) with documented comment period >= 14 days, 100% issue press releases >= 5 days before trial, 100% publish GDPR Privacy Notice prior to trial start, 100% document engagement activities (notices, attendance, comments, responses) and provide summary to CAA >= 7 days before trial start.
pass_threshold: 100% of trials identify all landowners within 2 km (via land registry); 100% deliver written notices >= 30 days prior with proof of delivery; 100% conduct community consultation (in-person or online) with >= 14-day comment period; all concerns documented and responses provided; 100% issue media press releases >= 5 days before trial; 100% publish GDPR Privacy Notice describing personal data collection, retention (2 years), data subject rights, DPO contact; 100% compile engagement summary submitted to CAA >= 7 days before trial start
sample_size_or_coverage: 5 representative trial areas (mix of rural, suburban, urban locations; mix of landowner density - sparse to complex); landowner identification audit: land registry records checked for all 5 areas; community consultation documentation: notices, event attendance records/online portal logs, concern comments; media documentation: press releases issued, media coverage tracked; GDPR Privacy Notice review: one notice per trial, verified against GDPR Article 13/14 checklist
test_owner: Applicant public-engagement team (notice delivery, consultation), CAA Programme Manager (engagement summary review), CAA Data Protection Officer (GDPR Privacy Notice review)
anchor_meter: METER-002 (Paragraph-Level Coverage Audit) adapted for engagement documentation completeness
anchor_sla: None directly; but engagement timelines (30 days notice, 14-day comment, 5-day media notice) are hard deadlines before trial authorization
notes: REQ017 missing from original user stories; added per Natasha feedback on stakeholder engagement gaps. Operationalized through 4-component engagement framework (Landowner Notification 30 days, Community Consultation 14 days, Media Relations 5 days, GDPR Privacy Notice). All activities documented with proof (notices, attendance records, concerns/responses, press releases). Acceptance is binary: all 4 components completed on schedule with documentation or fail. Closes CAP 1616 and GDPR compliance gaps.

requirement_title: Security Classification Handling Plan for Classified MOD Evidence
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: When trial involves classified MOD (Ministry of Defence) information or sensitive military technology, the Applicant shall establish a Security Classification Handling Plan (SCHP) per CAP 722H (CAA Security Management) and JSP 440 (UK Armed Forces Security Classification Policy). SCHP shall specify: (1) Classification Levels: identify which evidence elements are UK OFFICIAL (unclassified), UK OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE, UK SECRET, or UK TOP SECRET with JSP 440 justification, (2) Information Barriers: define what evidence CAA can access unclassified vs. what requires SECRET-level facility and clearance (e.g., swarm de-confliction algorithm public; sensor fusion specifications SECRET-caveat), (3) Redaction Rules: specify documents to be redacted for CAA review (unclassified summary of SORA assessment vs. classified sensor details), (4) Facility Requirements: identify where classified evidence stored/reviewed (CAA MOD Liaison Office with appropriate security infrastructure), (5) Personnel Clearances: specify which CAA personnel require security clearance (Director, designated technical reviewers) and which remain unclassified-access only, (6) Escalation: procedures for handling evidence CAA deems safety-critical but MAA classifies as restricted (CAA/MAA executive decision required). Applicant and MAA jointly draft SCHP and submit to CAA for approval prior to classified evidence submission. CAA designates Classified Evidence Reviewer (possessing required clearance) to conduct parallel assessment: (A) Classified assessment (full technical review), (B) Unclassified summary (findings and approval rationale safe for public record). Trial authorization decision may be issued on unclassified summary without disclosing classified details. Test shall be run on 5 representative classified MOD trials; pass criterion: 100% of trials have complete SCHP per CAP 722H and JSP 440, 100% SCHP approved by CAA Security Officer prior to evidence submission, 100% classified evidence handled per SCHP (stored, accessed, redacted correctly), 100% parallel assessments conducted (classified + unclassified summary), zero unauthorized disclosure of classified details.
pass_threshold: 100% SCHP documents complete per CAP 722H and JSP 440 checklist (classification levels justified, information barriers defined, redaction rules specified, facility/personnel/escalation documented); 100% approved by CAA Security Officer before evidence submission; 100% classified evidence physically stored in authorized facility (CAA MOD Liaison Office); 100% access limited to cleared personnel; 100% redaction rules applied correctly (verified via audit); 100% parallel assessments completed (classified technical review + unclassified summary for public record); zero unauthorized disclosures (security audit clean)
sample_size_or_coverage: 5 representative classified MOD trials (mix of classification levels UK OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE to UK TOP SECRET, mix of technologies - aerial vehicles, sensors, AI/autonomy, weapon systems); SCHP completeness audit: CAP 722H + JSP 440 checklist applied to all 5 plans; classified evidence handling audit: storage facility inspection, access logs reviewed, redaction spot-checks performed; disclosure prevention: security incident log reviewed (zero unauthorized disclosures)
test_owner: CAA Security Officer (SCHP approval), Classified Evidence Reviewer (parallel assessment), MAA Security Liaison (SCHP co-drafting), CAA Information Security team (facility audit, access control verification)
anchor_meter: None directly; security compliance task rather than technical verification
anchor_sla: SCHP approval must be completed >= 14 days before classified evidence submission to establish facilities and personnel clearances
notes: REQ018 missing from original user stories; added per Natasha feedback on classified information handling. Operationalized through 6-component SCHP framework per CAP 722H and JSP 440. Parallel assessment process (classified + unclassified) enables CAA to make trial decisions without public disclosure of classified details. Acceptance is binary: complete SCHP approved + zero unauthorized disclosures or fail. Closes classified-information security gap and enables CAA/MAA collaboration on sensitive trials.

requirement_title: Environmental Impact Assessment for Noise, Species Disturbance, and Battery Fire Risk
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: The Applicant shall conduct an Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) addressing noise, species disturbance, and battery/fuel fire risk. EIA shall include: (1) Noise Assessment: measure or model predicted noise levels (dB(A)) at sensitive receptors (residences, schools, hospitals) within 5 km for day (0700-2200) and night (2200-0700) operations. Noise shall not exceed: daytime 75 dB(A) at residential receivers, night 65 dB(A) per Environmental Protection Act 1990. If predicted noise exceeds limits, applicant proposes mitigation (flight path adjustment, noise baffles, operational restrictions to daylight only, lower altitude limits). (2) Species Disturbance Assessment: identify sensitive species (birds, bats, protected fauna) within 10 km using published ecology databases and expert consultation. Identify breeding seasons and dusk/night activity periods (highest disturbance risk for nocturnal species). Assess UAS noise/motion disturbance per wildlife sensitivity guidance. If disturbance risk elevated (e.g., nest sites within 1 km, bat foraging habitat within 2 km during breeding), propose mitigation (seasonal flight restrictions, dusk/night flight ban, artificial shelter/illumination). (3) Battery/Fuel Fire Risk: assess fire risk from UAS battery or fuel leakage on ground. Quantify: lithium battery cell capacity (Wh), thermal runaway risk, fire radius. Require certification that batteries meet IEC 62619 (lithium safety) and crash-landing area clear of vegetation/structures where fire could spread. Identify emergency fire suppression procedures (extinguisher type, crew training). Test shall be run on 5 representative trial areas; pass criterion: 100% predicted noise <= 75 dB(A) day / 65 dB(A) night at receptors (or mitigation documented), 100% sensitive species within 10 km identified with breeding-season constraints documented, 100% species disturbance risk assessed with mitigation or seasonal restrictions specified, 100% battery fire risk assessed with IEC 62619 compliance confirmed, 100% EIA signed by qualified experts (noise engineer, ecologist, fire-safety engineer).
pass_threshold: 100% noise assessments show predicted dB(A) levels <= 75 day / 65 night at sensitive receptors OR documented mitigation (flight path, baffles, daylight-only, altitude restrictions); 100% species assessments complete (ecology database search, breeding-season calendar, disturbance risk scoring); all high-risk species have specified mitigation (seasonal closure, night flight ban, shelter/lighting); 100% battery/fuel fire risk quantified (cell capacity, thermal runaway scenario, fire radius); 100% UAS batteries certified IEC 62619; 100% crash-landing areas assessed for fire propagation risk; 100% EIA signed by qualified experts (noise engineer, ecologist, fire-safety engineer credentials verified)
sample_size_or_coverage: 5 representative trial areas (mix of ecosystem sensitivity: urban/suburban with some protected species, rural with active breeding habitats, coastal with migratory species); noise assessment: measure or model for 10+ sensitive receptors per area; species assessment: 5+ species per area (birds, bats, ground-nesting species, aquatic species); fire risk: battery cell capacity range 10,000-50,000 Wh per area
test_owner: Applicant environmental-compliance team (EIA coordination), qualified noise engineer (noise assessment), qualified ecologist (species assessment), qualified fire-safety engineer (battery fire assessment), CAA Environmental Compliance Officer (review and approval)
anchor_meter: METER-002 (Paragraph-Level Coverage Audit) adapted for EIA section completeness (noise, species, fire risk sections all present and substantive)
anchor_sla: None directly; but EIA must be completed >= 30 days before trial authorization to allow time for mitigation planning and regulatory consultation
notes: REQ019 missing from original user stories; added per Natasha feedback on environmental-impact gaps. Operationalized through 3-component EIA framework (Noise, Species, Battery Fire). All acceptance criteria are numeric (dB(A) limits, species distance thresholds, IEC 62619 certification). 'Dusk/night' operations explicitly addressed as higher-risk period for species disturbance with specific mitigation (seasonal closure, night flight ban). Acceptance is binary: noise <= 75/65, species mitigation documented, battery certified IEC 62619 or fail. Closes environmental compliance gap.

requirement_title: Post-Trial Lessons-Learned, Evidence Preservation, Technical Debrief, and Authorisation Close-Out
acceptance_criterion_ieee830: Upon trial completion, the Applicant shall conduct Post-Trial Assurance and Close-out per the following requirements: (1) Lessons-Learned Workshop: within 30 days of final flight, convene CAA, applicant, crew, and independent technical reviewer to review: (A) all incidents/anomalies logged during trial, (B) unexpected behaviors observed (emergent swarm behaviors, crew workload surprises, equipment failures), (C) deviations from ConOps, (D) regulatory gate performance (meaningful gates?). Workshop produces written lessons-learned report (minimum 10 pages) documenting: findings, root causes (if incidents occurred), recommendations for future BVLOS swarm trials, applicant proposed corrective actions. (2) Evidence Preservation: all recorded evidence (telemetry, video, audio, logs per REQ012) preserved in digital archive with: (A) Format standardization (CSV telemetry, MP4 video, WAV audio), (B) Metadata documentation (flight date, aircraft ID, crew identifiers, trial phase), (C) Integrity verification (SHA256 hash of all files), (D) Retention period: minimum 2 years or until litigation/investigation closes, whichever longer. Archive transferred to CAA Safety Investigation department for long-term storage. (3) Technical Debrief: independent technical reviewer (not involved in trial) conducts forensic analysis of evidence to validate: swarm behavior remained within predicted bounds (per REQ008), human control maintained (per REQ010), no safety-critical near-miss scenarios missed. Reviewer issues report documenting findings and confidence (high/medium/low) for each validation. (4) Authorisation Close-out: CAA issues formal notice closing trial authorization. Notice specifies: (A) trial authorization no longer valid, (B) aircraft cease operations in trial configuration, (C) evidence received and preserved, (D) if applicable, recommendations for future BVLOS swarm authorizations (e.g., 'swarm de-confliction validated; acceptable for expanded swarm size in future trials'). Programme Manager signs close-out notice. Test shall be run on 3 representative trials; pass criterion: 100% of trials conduct lessons-learned workshop within 30 days, 100% produce lessons-learned reports (minimum 10 pages with findings, root causes, recommendations), 100% preserve all evidence with SHA256 verification, 100% conduct independent technical debrief with confidence scoring, 100% issue formal CAA close-out notice with recommendations for future trials.
pass_threshold: 100% of trials complete lessons-learned workshop within 30 days of final flight with CAA, applicant, crew, independent reviewer attendance documented; 100% produce lessons-learned report >= 10 pages documenting findings, root causes (if applicable), recommendations, applicant corrective actions; 100% evidence archived with standardized format (CSV, MP4, WAV), metadata documented, SHA256 hashes verified; 100% preserved for >= 2 years; 100% independent technical debrief completed with confidence (high/medium/low) scoring for behavior bounds, human control, near-miss detection; 100% formal CAA close-out notice issued with trial-deauthorization statement and recommendations for future trials
sample_size_or_coverage: 3 representative trials (trial 1: successful completion no incidents, trial 2: minor incidents/anomalies occurred, trial 3: encountered contingency/reversal); lessons-learned audit: 3 workshop reports reviewed for completeness (findings, root causes, recommendations); evidence preservation audit: all telemetry, video, audio, logs from 3 trials archived and SHA256-verified; technical debrief audit: independent assessments completed for all 3 trials with confidence scoring; close-out audit: CAA formal notices issued for all 3 trials with authorization statements and future-trial recommendations
test_owner: CAA Programme Manager (workshop facilitation, close-out authority), independent technical reviewer (debrief conduct, confidence scoring), CAA Safety Investigation department (evidence archival and long-term storage), applicant team (workshop participation, lessons-learned documentation)
anchor_meter: METER-002 (Paragraph-Level Coverage Audit) adapted for lessons-learned report completeness (findings, root causes, recommendations sections all present and substantive)
anchor_sla: None directly; but workshops must be completed within 30 days of final flight to capture institutional memory while fresh
notes: REQ020 missing from original user stories; added per Natasha feedback on post-trial governance and continuous-learning gaps. Operationalized through 4-component framework (Lessons-Learned, Evidence Preservation, Technical Debrief, Close-out). Lessons-learned report is scoped to minimum 10 pages with 4 required sections (findings, root causes, recommendations, corrective actions). Evidence preservation is operationalized with format standards (CSV, MP4, WAV), metadata requirements, SHA256 verification, 2-year retention. Technical debrief uses confidence scoring (high/medium/low) to capture reviewer assessment quality. Close-out notice formalizes trial deauthorization and captures recommendations for future trials (important for institutional learning across BVLOS swarm trials). Acceptance is binary: all 4 components completed on schedule with documented quality or fail. Closes post-trial assurance gap and supports continuous improvement in regulatory process.
